name: FastSpring E-Commerce API
what_it_does: FastSpring REST API for full-service e-commerce platform specializing in software and SaaS sales. Enables AI agents to manage orders, subscriptions, products, and coupons; retrieve revenue analytics; process refunds; and integrate FastSpring into SaaS billing and digital goods sales workflows. FastSpring is a merchant of record, handling taxes, compliance, and payment processing globally.
use_cases: Managing software subscriptions and license orders from SaaS billing agents
Retrieving revenue and order analytics from finance operations agents
Processing refunds and subscription changes from customer support agents
Integrating FastSpring order data into CRM and fulfillment workflow agents
not_for: Physical product sales (FastSpring is digital goods focused)
Non-software digital commerce without licensing needs
best_when: A software company using FastSpring as merchant of record wants AI agents to manage subscriptions, process orders, and retrieve revenue analytics for their digital products.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: Order management errors can cause incorrect billing, missed renewals, and revenue loss. FastSpring is merchant of record — errors have real customer financial impact.

auth.notes: FastSpring API uses HTTP Basic authentication with username (store path) and API key as password. API keys created in FastSpring dashboard. Account-level access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — FastSpring takes percentage of revenue
pricing.paid_tiers: Percentage-based pricing (varies by plan and volume)
pricing.notes: FastSpring is a merchant of record — revenue share model rather than flat subscription. Contact FastSpring for pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per account; contact FastSpring support for limits

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Order processing errors affect real customer billing — use sandbox for testing
No public MCP server — REST API integration required
FastSpring is merchant of record — tax, compliance, and payment processing included but complex
Store path (storefront identifier) required in all API calls
Basic auth with API key as password — not OAuth; less granular access control
Webhook payloads require HMAC signature verification to prevent spoofing
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Revenue and order data. PCI-DSS compliant as merchant of record. GDPR, SOC2. Basic auth — no granular scope control.

security_score_components.security_notes: Revenue and order data. PCI-DSS as MoR. GDPR, SOC2. Basic auth with API key.
